_comment: Paperclip benchmark #15 — active model evaluator. Per-role suites -> multi-model runner -> deterministic+LLM-judge scorer -> quality-per-token -> per-role model recommendation that drives data-driven tiering (#9). Models are invoked via their CLIs in a neutralized temp CWD (no project CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/rules) so we measure the base model, not the local agent's tools/skills (that is #16's job).
_models_catalog_comment: Validated within-family variants (all confirmed callable via their CLI 2026-06-14). NOT in the default sweep — `bench.py all` runs `models` above. Opt in per run: `bench.py all --models claude-haiku,gpt-5.4-mini,gemini-flash,grok-4-fast`. These are the 'lower model' candidates for the cheap-model-+-skill gap-closing strategy. (codex gpt-5.3-codex-spark / gpt-5-codex are NOT callable via `codex exec -m` — adapter-profile/spark-exhausted.)

judge._comment: Judge = claude-opus (user authorized heavy claude use 2026-06-14). Sharpest available grader and most neutral (it is a contestant too, but the judge is blind to which model produced an output and applied uniformly, so rankings are fair; its mild self-preference is the one caveat). The gemini-judge baseline (run-20260614-044003) is archived for a cross-judge agreement check. Swap back to {"id":"gemini-pro","adapter":"gemini","model_arg":null} to conserve claude quota.

scoring._comment: Per-task blend. If a task has only deterministic checks (e.g. intake ground-truth), quality = deterministic. If only judge criteria, quality = judge. If both, quality = normalized weighted blend of the two.

recommendation._comment: objective=peak_quality_cost_aware (davin's policy 2026-06-14): TIER FOR PEAK QUALITY, but don't pay multiples for marginal gains. Rule per role: start from the highest-quality model; among models within quality_epsilon (0.02) of it, pick the one with the LOWEST mean output tokens — BUT only drop from the peak if the cheaper model is materially cheaper (peak_output / cheaper_output >= cost_ratio_trigger, default 1.5x). Otherwise keep the peak. Report shows peak, the cost-aware pick, quality given up, and the output-cost ratio so the tradeoff is explicit. Set quality_epsilon=0 to always take true peak. value_metric=output because total tokens are ~95% fixed CLI overhead (a harness artifact).

run._comment: Each model call runs in a fresh empty temp CWD with config/rules-ignore flags where the CLI supports them. max_workers parallelizes across (role,task,model) cells; keep modest — the Mac is shared with the live fleet + image-gen.
